perturbation.original_text: The party disclosing Confidential Informa tion shall be considered the "Discloser." The party receiving Confidential Information shall be considered the "Recipient."
perturbation.changed_text: The party disclosing Confidential Informa tion shall be considered the "Provider." The party receiving Confidential Information shall be considered the "Beneficiary."
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Discloser' to 'Provider' and 'Recipient' to 'Beneficiary' introduces confusion because the rest of the document consistently uses 'Discloser' and 'Recipient'. This creates an in-text contradiction as terms defined here are not maintained, especially in sections detailing obligations and rights of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Discloser’s Confidential Information is a special, valuable and a unique asset, and agrees that for a period of three (3) years following the receipt of the Confidential Information it shall: (a) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without written consent of Disclos er, (b) restrict dissemination of Confidential Information to only its employees, contractors, or agents who are directly participating in the Authorized Purpose, who have a need to know the Confidential Information, and who are bound by a duty of confid entiality under terms no less re strictive than contained herein concerning the use of Confidential Information, and (c) use the same degree of care as for its own information of like importance, but at least reasonable care, in preventing disclosu re of Confidential Information. Recipient further agrees not to reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble any prototypes, soft ware, hardware or other tangible objects or products provided hereunder which embody the Confidential Information of the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Discloser' to 'Provider' and 'Recipient' to 'Beneficiary' directly contradicts the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text explicitly uses 'Recipient' and 'Discloser' and defines obligations based on these terms. The changed text's modification makes it unclear who is obligated under the 'Recipient' obligations in the Contradicted Text. This creates an in-text contradiction as the defined terms are not maintained throughout the document.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed during the ON Semiconductor Industry Analyst Conference to be held by invitation only on April 18-20, 2011 , in Scottsdale, Arizona, hereunder may only be used for the following purpose: For evaluation and discussion of market tr ends and data refinement in the areas of ON Semiconductor’s focus market segments. (hereinafter “Authorized Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed during the ON Semiconductor Industry Analyst Conference to be held by invitation only on April 18-20, 2011 , in Scottsdale, Arizona, hereunder may only be used for the following purpose: For general informational purposes. (hereinafter “Authorized Purpose”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'For evaluation and discussion of market tr ends and data refinement in the areas of ON Semiconductor’s focus market segments' to 'For general informational purposes' broadens the definition of 'Authorized Purpose'. Later clauses restrict the use of information to the 'Authorized Purpose', making it unclear if general use is permitted, directly contradicting the intent of restricting usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information may be exchanged between the parties under this Agreement to the extent necessary to fulfill the Authorized Purpose, and shall not be used for any other purpose. Recipient acknowledges that the Discloser’s Confidential Information is a special, valuable and a unique asset, and agrees that for a period of three (3) years following the receipt of the Confidential Information it shall: (a) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without written consent of Disclos er, (b) restrict dissemination of Confidential Information to only its employees, contractors, or agents who are directly participating in the Authorized Purpose, who have a need to know the Confidential Information, and who are bound by a duty of confid entiality under terms no less re strictive than contained herein concerning the use of Confidential Information, and (c) use the same degree of care as for its own information of like importance, but at least reasonable care, in preventing disclosu re of Confidential Information. Recipient further agrees not to reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble any prototypes, soft ware, hardware or other tangible objects or products provided hereunder which embody the Confidential Information of the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted the use of confidential information to 'evaluation and discussion of market trends and data refinement'. The changed text broadens this to 'For general informational purposes'. The contradicted text states that Confidential Information 'shall not be used for any other purpose' than the 'Authorized Purpose'. The change makes the Authorized Purpose very broad ('general informational purposes'), which then conflicts with the restriction in the contradicted text against using the information for any other purpose. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text permits a general use, while the contradicted text prohibits uses beyond the authorized purpose, which was initially more specific.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Discloser’s Confidential Information is a special, valuable and a unique asset, and agrees that for a period of three (3) years following the receipt of the Confidential Information it shall: (a) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without written consent of Disclos er, (b) restrict dissemination of Confidential Information to only its employees, contractors, or agents who are directly participating in the Authorized Purpose, who have a need to know the Confidential Information, and who are bound by a duty of confid entiality under terms no less re strictive than contained herein concerning the use of Confidential Information, and (c) use the same degree of care as for its own information of like importance, but at least reasonable care, in preventing disclosu re of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Discloser’s Confidential Information is a useful piece of information, and agrees that for a period of one (1) year following the receipt of the Confidential Information it shall: (a) disclose the Confidential Information to any third party with the Discloser's consent, (b) allow dissemination of Confidential Information to all its employees, contractors, or agents, who have a need to know the Confidential Information, and (c) use minimum care, in preventing disclosu re of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The changes made to section 3 directly contradict the purpose of an NDA. Reducing the protection period from three years to one year, changing 'not disclose' to 'disclose', allowing widespread dissemination, and reducing the care required for protection create a direct contradiction to the agreement's core.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that damages for improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary inj unction, in addition to all other remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction with the contradicted text. The original NDA aimed to protect confidential information, seeking injunctions for improper disclosure. The changed text weakens the protection by shortening the protection period, allowing disclosure with consent, permitting widespread dissemination, and reducing the required care. This directly contradicts the intent to protect confidential information and the clause about irreparable damage, making equitable relief less plausible. The changes create a strong and direct conflict within the document, fitting the definition of misaligned terminology leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon Recipient with respect to Confidential Information which is: (a) is or becomes generally known or publicly available through no act or failure on the part of Recipient, or (b) approved in writing by the Discloser for public release or disclosure by the Recipient; or (c) disclosed to a third party by Discloser without a duty of confidentiality or is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without a duty of confidentialit y or restriction on disclosure, or (d) independently known by or independently developed by the Recipient without the use of Confidential Information disclosed by the Discloser; or (e) is required to be disclosed pursuant to the order of a court of competent ju risdiction; or otherwise required to be disclosed by law through no act of the Recipient, provided, ho wever, that the Recipient has notified the Discloser upon learning of the possibility that disclosure could be required pursuant to any such law or legal order and has given the Discloser a reasonable opportunity to c ontest or limit the scope of such requir ed disclosure and has cooperated with the Discloser toward this end.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon Recipient with respect to Confidential Information which is: (a) is or becomes generally known or publicly available, or (b) approved in writing by the Discloser for public release or disclosure by the Recipient; or (c) disclosed to a third party or is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party, or (d) independently known by or independently developed by the Recipient, or (e) is required to be disclosed pursuant to the order of a court of competent ju risdiction.
perturbation.explanation: Removing clauses like 'through no act or failure on the part of Recipient', 'without a duty of confidentiality or restriction on disclosure' and 'without the use of Confidential Information disclosed by the Discloser' from section 4 makes the exceptions much broader. The recipient is no longer liable if they caused the information to become public or obtained it unlawfully, contradicting the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that damages for improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary inj unction, in addition to all other remedies.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided several conditions under which the recipient would not be obligated to maintain confidentiality. The changed text removes key limitations on these exceptions, such as 'through no act or failure on the part of Recipient' and 'without a duty of confidentiality or restriction on disclosure'. This broadened exception directly conflicts with the 'Contradicted Text', which states that parties are liable for 'improper disclosure of Confidential Information'. By removing the original limitations, the recipient is no longer held liable if they were responsible for the disclosure, thus creating a contradiction. The explanation of how the change creates a contradiction is valid.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate thirty (30) days after the effect ive date of this Agreement. Termination shall not, however, affect the rights and obligations included herein with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder prior to termination. Upon termination of this Agreement, each party will, upon request of the Discloser, and within a reasonable period of time thereafter, return all C onfidential Information received from the Discloser and copies made thereof by the Recipient, or, if acceptable to the Disclos er, certify by written memorandum that all such Confidential Information has been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate immediately after the effect ive date of this Agreement. Termination shall affect the rights and obligations included herein with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder prior to termination. Upon termination of this Agreement, each party will not, upon request of the Discloser, return all C onfidential Information received from the Discloser and copies made thereof by the Recipient, or, certify by written memorandum that all such Confidential Information has been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'terminate thirty (30) days after' to 'terminate immediately after' and stating termination *does* affect rights and obligations, coupled with *no return* of confidential information directly contradicts the standard practice of NDAs, especially concerning the preservation of confidentiality after termination and reverses the intent of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entir e understanding between the parties relative to the protection of Confidential Information and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports, and understandings between the parties in respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the original agreement regarding confidential information. The original text states that the agreement terminates 30 days after the effective date and obligations related to confidential information survive termination. The changed text modifies this to immediate termination, stating that termination *does* affect these rights and obligations, and that confidential information will *not* be returned. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which describes the agreement as the *entire understanding* regarding the protection of Confidential Information. If termination immediately ends confidentiality obligations and information doesn't need to be returned as the changed text describes, that directly contradicts that the agreement is the entire understanding relative to the protection of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The law of the state of New York, U.S.A. except for its choice of laws rules shall govern this Agreement. The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys fees and costs. The Recipient agrees that, in addition to all other remedies, the Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of the obligations herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The law of the state of Delaware, U.S.A. including for its choice of laws rules shall govern this Agreement. The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement shall not be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys fees and costs. The Recipient agrees that, in addition to all other remedies, the Discloser shall not be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of the obligations herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from New York to Delaware (including its choice of law rules), removing the right to recover attorney's fees, and preventing the discloser from obtaining specific performance makes the agreement significantly weaker and undermines the discloser's ability to enforce its rights, contradicting the intent of protecting confidential information effectively.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that damages for improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary inj unction, in addition to all other remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the discloser's ability to enforce their rights by changing the governing law, removing the right to recover attorney's fees, and preventing specific performance. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary injunction, in addition to all other remedies for improper disclosure of confidential information. Removing equitable relief directly contradicts the availability of such relief outlined in the contradicted text.
